scenario: Tasklist shows 100 tasks per user. Sorting default seems random. Wonders sort options.
question: Tasklist task sorting?

options.text: Camunda Tasklist does not support any task sorting capabilities and always displays tasks in arbitrary order â€” this approach has limitations. Camunda 8 Tasklist provides configurable task list sorting with multiple ordering attributes: creation date (newest first or oldest first), due date (earliest due first), follow-up date, priority (highest first), and completion date. Users can select their preferred sort order from the Tasklist UI. Additionally, the Tasklist REST API supports orderBy and sort parameters for programmatically controlling task list ordering in custom applications.

md:69-77: Date of creation Due date Follow-up date (latest time work should START; distinct from due date which is the DEADLINE) Priority (per Set 15 Q12) Triggered via the order icon in the UI. 8 Tasklist UI. md:58-59 -- they DON'T sync across browsers/devices. md:19-25: All open tasks / Assigned to me / Unassigned / Completed tasks. 8 path -- legacy /v1/tasks/search is the older Tasklist API).

8 supports sorting by: Date of creation, Due date, Follow-up date, and Priority -- none of which are alphabetical by name. An alphabetical sort by task name is not one of the four documented ordering options. The supported orderings are temporal (creation, due date, follow-up date) and priority-based, reflecting the work management focus of Tasklist where time sensitivity and business priority drive task triage, not alphabetical naming.

Task ordering in Camunda 8 Tasklist is random -- there is no deterministic sort order -- this approach has limitations. 8 provides four documented ordering attributes (Date of creation, Due date, Follow-up date, Priority) accessible via the order icon in the UI. The REST API POST /user-tasks/search endpoint also supports programmatic ordering with additional flexibility. While there may be a default display order when no explicit sort is selected, calling it "random" is inaccurate -- it follows a deterministic default order (typically creation date descending). Claiming random ordering would make Tasklist unusable as a task management tool.

a.text: Incorrect. Tasklist sort options: Standard sort criteria (verify per version): Creation date: Newest first: see recent tasks. Oldest first: address backlog. Due date: Most urgent first: prioritise. Optional: tasks without due date last. Priority (per Set 15 Q12): High to low: most urgent. Numeric: per task priority attribute. Assignee state: Assigned to me vs Available. Claimed vs Open. Task name / process: Alphabetical: by task / process name. Group by process: cluster tasks of same type. Variable-based sort is NOT a documented 8.8 Tasklist UI feature -- sort menu exposes only the 4 attributes above. For variable-driven ordering, build a custom UI on the Search user tasks REST API. UX features: Multi-sort: Primary sort + secondary sort. E.g., priority desc, then creation date asc. Persistent: User's last sort preference saved. Don't reset each session. Per-view sorting: My tasks: sort by priority. Group tasks: sort by creation date. Different views, different defaults. Filtering combined with sort: Filter: which tasks to show. Sort: order of shown. Combined: focused list. For the scenario (100 tasks, random sort): Steps: 1. Check Tasklist version: features evolve. 2. Enable / configure sort: use UI controls. 3. Set default sort: most useful for users. 4. Train users: how to sort. Common sort recommendations: For prioritised work: priority desc + due date asc. For oldest backlog: creation date asc. For most recent: creation date desc. Custom Tasklist UI: If standard sort insufficient: build custom. Tasklist API: query with custom sort. Full control: business-specific logic. API-based sorting (Orchestration Cluster REST API, 8.8 path): ` POST /v2/user-tasks/search Content-Type: application/json { "sort": [ { "field": "priority", "order": "DESC" }, { "field": "creationDate", "order": "ASC" } ] } Multiple sort criteria supported via the sort` array. See Search user tasks (https://docs.camunda.io/docs/apis-tools/orchestration-cluster-api-rest/specifications/search-user-tasks/) for the full schema. For accessibility: Keyboard navigation: tab through tasks. Screen readers: announce sort order. For team consistency: Default sort chosen by team / org. Document recommendation: in onboarding. For SLA enforcement: Sort by due date. Operators address most urgent first. Best practices: Choose sensible defaults: based on typical workflow. Allow user override: persistent. Document sort options: training. Test on real data: ensure useful. Anti-patterns: No default sort: random; users confused. Single fixed sort: doesn't fit all users. Resetting on every login: annoying. For monitoring: Track task age by sort metrics. Old tasks accumulating → tune sort.
